mission.title: k3s: Missing network metrics for containers
mission.description: Missing network metrics for containers. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current k3s deployment
steps.description:
Verify your k3s version and configuration:
```bash
k3s --version
kubectl get nodes
```
This feature requires a working k3s installation.

steps.title: Review k3s configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant k3s configuration:
```bash
k3s --version
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
```
K3s Version: 1.24.3+k3s1

Node(s) CPU architecture, OS, and Version: 
Linux 5.18.10-200.fc36.aarch64 aarch64 GNU/Linux
Linux 5.18.10-200.fc36.x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

Cluster Configuration: 3 servers, 1 agent

I am missing some metrics in grafana,

steps.title: Apply the fix for Missing network metrics for containers
steps.description:
Hmm. The metrics output you shared seems to show that the metrics are present and non-zero. Is there something in particular that's missing? Have you confirmed that you're scraping those metrics? Are you sure the problem is not your prometheus query?
```yaml
---
# https://rancher.com/docs/k3s/latest/en/installation/install-options/server-config/
# https://github.com/PyratLabs/ansible-role-k3s

# (bool) Specify if a host (or host group) are part of the control plane
k3s_control_node: true

# (dict) k3s settings for all control-plane nodes
k3s_server:
  node-ip: "{{ ansible_host }}"
  tls-san:
    # kube-vip
    - "{{ kubevip_address }}"
  # Disable Docker - this will use the default containerd CRI
  docker: false
  node-taint:
    - "node-role.kubernetes.io/master=true:NoSchedule"
  flannel-backend: "none" # This needs to be in quotes
  disable:
  
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
kubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Missing network metrics for containers" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Hmm. The metrics output you shared seems to show that the metrics are present and non-zero. Is there something in particular that's missing? Have you confirmed that you're scraping those metrics? Are you sure the problem is not your prometheus query?
codeSnippets:
---
# https://rancher.com/docs/k3s/latest/en/installation/install-options/server-config/
# https://github.com/PyratLabs/ansible-role-k3s

# (bool) Specify if a host (or host group) are part of the control plane
k3s_control_node: true

# (dict) k3s settings for all control-plane nodes
k3s_server:
  node-ip: "{{ ansible_host }}"
  tls-san:
    # kube-vip
    - "{{ kubevip_address }}"
  # Disable Docker - this will use the default containerd CRI
  docker: false
  node-taint:
    - "node-role.kubernetes.io/master=true:NoSchedule"
  flannel-backend: "none" # This needs to be in quotes
  disable:
    # Disable flannel - replaced with Calico
    - flannel
    # Disable traefik - replaced with ingress-nginx
    - traefik
    # Disable servicelb - replaced with metallb and install with Flux
    - s

---
# https://rancher.com/docs/k3s/latest/en/installation/install-options/agent-config/
# https://github.com/PyratLabs/ansible-role-k3s

# (bool) Specify if a host (or host group) are part of the control plane
k3s_control_node: false

# (dict) k3s settings for all worker nodes
k3s_agent:
  node-ip: "{{ ansible_host }}"
  kubelet-arg:
    # Allow k8s services to contain TCP and UDP on the same port
    - "feature-gates=MixedProtocolLBService=true"
    # Fix for metrics
    # https://github.com/k3s-io/k3s/issues/473
    - "containerd=/run/k3s/containerd/containerd.sock"

# Drop cgroup metrics with no container.
      - sourceLabels: [id, container]
        action: drop
        regex: '.+;'

prerequisites.description: A running k3s cluster or a system where k3s can be installed.
